perturbation.original_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of three (3 ) years from the effective date of thi s Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of three (3 ) years from the effective date of thi s Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to unauthorized parties or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'others' to 'unauthorized parties'. The term 'others' is broad, but 'unauthorized parties' introduces subjectivity because unauthorized party wasn't defined, and could lead to arguments about who is considered authorized, weakening the protection of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Notwithstanding anything to the c ontrary in Paragraph 4 hereof, Proprietary Information may be disclosed by a receiving Party to those of its employees and consultants who require knowledge thereof in connection with their duties in conducting the aforesaid purpose of this Agreement and w ho are obligated by written agreement to hold such Proprietary Information in confidence and restrict its use consistent with the receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement; and Proprietary Information may be disclosed to a legislative, judicial, o r regulatory body requiring its disclosure, provided that, prior to such disclosure, the receiving Party has notified the disclosing Party of the requirement with an opportunity for the disclosing Party to object or seek an appropriate protective order.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'others' to 'unauthorized parties' in Paragraph 4 introduces subjectivity by using a term ('unauthorized parties') that is not defined in the contract. This weakens the protection of Proprietary Information. The contradicted text, Paragraph 7, allows disclosure to employees and consultants obligated by written agreement, and to legislative, judicial, or regulatory bodies. By narrowing the scope of non-disclosure to 'unauthorized parties,' the change creates ambiguity and potential conflict with Paragraph 7, as it's unclear who now falls under the definition of 'unauthorized parties' which could include employees and consultants, leading to uncertainty and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within fifteen (15) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which th e disclosure occurred and the general nature of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within thirty (30) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which th e disclosure occurred and the specific details of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'fifteen (15) days' to 'thirty (30) days' and 'general nature of the information disclosed' to 'specific details of the information disclosed'. Contradicts the original timeframe for confirming oral disclosures, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over timely confirmation of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within thirty (30) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which th e disclosure occurred and the specific details of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'fifteen (15) days' for confirming oral disclosures, while the changed text modifies this to 'thirty (30) days'. The contradicted text also states 'thirty (30) days' which makes it appear there is no contradiction. However, the perturbation explanation states that there is a contradiction because changing 'fifteen (15) days' to 'thirty (30) days' contradicts the original timeframe for confirming oral disclosures, creating uncertainty. The definition of Misaligned Terminology indicates that misaligned terminology leads to **in-text contradictions** when the contract **uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them**, leading to conflicting obligations. In this scenario, the terms are 'fifteen (15) days' vs 'thirty (30 days)' and it creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied documents must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the supplied data' to 'the supplied documents'. This alters the scope of what must be maintained and protected after the agreement terminates. 'Data' is much broader, and 'Documents' is more restrictive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "PROPRIETARY Information" shall mean, in the case of Excelerate and the Second Party, valuable data or confidential information that is owned or controlled by each party relating to any solicitation or request for proposal . Each Party's P roprietary Information may include, but is not limited to, patents, copyrights, design methods, ideas, concepts, data, formulas, manufacturing techniques, know -how, business plans, customer lists, solicitation response strategies, technical solutions to cl ient requirements, system architectures, proposal preparation techniques and pricing policies, software, methodologies, technologies, processes, financial information, and sales and marketing information. Edit the highlighted portion of the first sentence in this paragraph to reflect the activities that are part of the NDA then delete this sentence.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that 'supplied data' must be maintained and protected. The changed text narrows this to 'supplied documents'. The contradicted text defines 'PROPRIETARY Information' as including a broad range of data, including data, business plans, software, methodologies, technologies, processes, financial information, etc. By changing 'data' to 'documents,' the modified text creates a contradiction because it implies that only the documents, and not the broader range of data defined in the contradicted section, are subject to the maintenance and protection obligations after the agreement terminates. This alteration creates a clear conflict and impacts the scope of post-termination obligations, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
